  • Angular Story
  • Get in touch
PL EN

Angular Evolution I've Experienced

A Journey from Version 8 to Version 20

Angular 8 Stability and Differential Loading

Key Features

  • Differential loading Modern and legacy browsers get separate bundles.
    Newer devices download lighter code faster.
    Better startup time without losing compatibility.
  • Dynamic imports for lazy routes Lazy routes load only when users need them.
    The initial application bundle becomes smaller.
    Navigation in large apps feels smoother.
  • Web Worker support via CLI Heavy computations can run off the main UI thread.
    The interface stays responsive during processing.
    It is easier to handle expensive tasks in background.
  • Builder API improvements You gain better control over the build process.
    Pipeline customization becomes more straightforward.
    Fewer workarounds and custom CI scripts are needed.

Deprecated Features

  • Old Http module treated as legacy
  • Node 8 support phased out
ng update @angular/cli@8 @angular/core@8 // Differential loading enabled by default
🧩 Angular 8

Angular 9 Ivy by Default

Key Features

  • Ivy compiler as default The new compiler is enabled by default in new projects.
    Generated code is more optimized.
    Build and test runs usually finish faster.
  • Improved AOT and type checking Template errors are caught earlier at compile time.
    There are fewer runtime surprises in production.
    Large components are easier to maintain safely.
  • Smaller bundle sizes The application downloads less data on startup.
    This is especially visible on slower connections.
    First render responsiveness improves.
  • Faster testing workflows Unit tests execute more efficiently.
    Teams get feedback in a shorter loop.
    Daily iterations become faster and smoother.

Deprecated Features

  • View Engine placed on deprecation path
  • Older renderer APIs started phasing out
ng version // Angular CLI: 9.x // Ivy: enabled
⚙️ Angular 9

Angular 10 Initial Foundation

Key Features

  • TypeScript 3.9 support Fewer false positives during compilation.
    Faster type checking in larger projects.
    Easier refactors without breaking the build.
  • Zone.js improvements More stable UI change detection.
    Fewer unexpected component re-renders.
    Better performance in complex views.
  • New date range picker component Quick implementation of date range selection.
    Consistent UX without building a custom widget.
    Less code needed for form handling.
  • Improved language service Better code hints in the editor.
    Faster navigation across templates and types.
    Less time spent debugging typos.

Deprecated Features

  • TSLint support starts to phase out
  • Legacy browser support marked for removal
ng version // Output: Angular 10.0.0
📦 Angular 10 Release

Angular 11 Improved Workflows

Major Updates

  • Automatic font inlining Fonts load faster on initial page render.
    Fewer extra requests to external resources.
    Improved Lighthouse rendering score.
  • Component testing utilities Simpler component test setup.
    Easier mocking of dependencies and inputs.
    More confidence when changing the UI.
  • Angular DevTools released Inspect the component tree in the browser.
    Quickly spot performance bottlenecks.
    Better change detection diagnostics.
  • Ivy engine optimizations Lighter production bundles.
    Faster startup on first load.
    Smoother local and CI builds.

Deprecated Features

  • Support for Webpack 4 based flows deprecated
  • Legacy i18n extraction workflow superseded
ng update @angular/cli @angular/core
🔧 Angular 11 Tools

Angular 12 Ivy as Default

Major Changes

  • Ivy engine becomes default New projects use the new engine by default.
    Better optimization of compiler-generated code.
    Fewer differences between dev and prod.
  • Support for TypeScript 4.2+ Modern language features are available immediately.
    Stronger typing in domain-level code.
    Easier maintenance of large data models.
  • Strict mode by default More issues are caught before runtime.
    Safer templates and component contracts.
    Higher code quality in new modules.
  • Build pipeline cleanup Fewer legacy steps in the build pipeline.
    Cleaner development tooling configuration.
    Easier migration to later Angular versions.

Deprecated Features

  • View Engine officially deprecated
  • IE11 support enters final deprecation window
// Angular 12 - Strict mode { "angularCompilerOptions": { "strictTemplates": true } }
⚙️ Ivy Engine

Angular 13 RxJS 7 & Ivy Enhancements

Significant Updates

  • RxJS 7 requirement New operators and more consistent stream APIs.
    Simpler subscription and cleanup management.
    Fewer edge cases in async logic.
  • IE11 support ended No need to maintain legacy polyfills.
    Smaller bundles and cleaner frontend code.
    Better focus on modern browsers.
  • New form control StatusChanges React faster to control state changes.
    Handle pending, valid, and invalid states easily in UI.
    Forms become more interactive and predictable.
  • Improved build performance Shorter local compile times.
    Faster feedback loop during development.
    More efficient CI/CD pipelines.

Deprecated Features

  • View Engine rendering pipeline removed
  • IE11 targeted polyfill strategy deprecated
// RxJS 7 syntax observable$.pipe( takeUntil(destroy$) ).subscribe(data => {});
📡 RxJS 7

Angular 14 Standalone Components

Paradigm Shift

  • Standalone components (preview) Build components without heavy NgModule setup.
    Easier to split the app into smaller units.
    Faster onboarding for new features.
  • Typed reactive forms FormControl and FormGroup use strict typing.
    Fewer mistakes when reading form values.
    Better IDE support for validations.
  • Enhanced routing API Route configuration becomes more declarative.
    Easier lazy loading and guard setup.
    Better readability in large applications.
  • Meta package release Simplified Angular package management.
    Easier framework dependency updates.
    Fewer version conflicts in the workspace.

Deprecated Features

  • Class based guards and resolvers deprecated
  • Old typed-forms workarounds no longer recommended
@Component({ standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], template: '...' }) export class MyComponent {}
🎯 Standalone APIs

Angular 15 Standalone by Default

Production Ready

  • Standalone components stable Use standalone APIs safely in production.
    Application architecture becomes lighter.
    Less boilerplate in new business modules.
  • Improved dependency injection Improved service injection ergonomics.
    Easier control of provider scopes.
    More predictable application behavior.
  • New control flow syntax preview Templates become easier for teams to read.
    Fewer nested structural directives.
    Faster authoring of conditions and loops.
  • Enhanced TypeScript 4.8 support Better compatibility with newer toolchains.
    More fixes for unknown type narrowing.
    More stable builds with newer TypeScript.

Deprecated Features

  • NgModule-first project scaffolding deprecated
  • Legacy builder defaults superseded
@Component({ standalone: true, selector: 'app-root' }) export class AppComponent {}
🚀 Standalone Ready

Angular 16 Signals & Reactivity

Major Features

  • Angular Signals released Reactivity becomes more predictable.
    Less manual subscription handling in components.
    Better performance with frequent state updates.
  • New control flow syntax Conditions and loops are shorter and clearer.
    Templates feel closer to modern app code.
    Easier code reviews and view maintenance.
  • Hydration support SSR becomes interactive faster on the client.
    Less content flicker after page load.
    Better Core Web Vitals for the app.
  • Required input properties Components immediately report missing required inputs.
    Integration issues are caught during development.
    Component APIs become clearer and stricter.

Deprecated Features

  • Zone.js-centric reactivity patterns deprecated
  • Constructor param decorators for inputs deprecated
import { signal } from '@angular/core'; count = signal(0); increment() { this.count.update(c => c + 1); }
⚡ Signals

Angular 17 Control Flow & DI

Developer Experience

  • New control flow (@if, @for, @switch) Templates are shorter and closer to JS logic.
    Fewer star-prefixed directives for simple cases.
    The code is easier for newcomers to learn.
  • Deferred loading with @defer Load heavy sections only when needed.
    Initial rendering becomes noticeably faster.
    Users see critical content sooner.
  • Stable hydration support SSR and CSR work more reliably in production.
    Fewer mismatches between HTML and app state.
    Better user experience after page refresh.
  • Improved CLI performance Faster project generation and builds.
    Shorter wait times in daily workflows.
    More efficient development iterations.

Deprecated Features

  • Structural directives for basic control flow deprecated
  • Some decorator-based query APIs marked for deprecation
@if (isVisible) { <p>Visible</p> } @else { <p>Hidden</p> }
🎨 Control Flow

Angular 18 Zoneless & Performance

Cutting Edge

  • Zoneless Angular (preview) Reduce dependency on the Zone.js mechanism.
    Gain better control over UI update cycles.
    Potentially lower runtime overhead.
  • Signal-based APIs New state APIs align with Signals.
    Easier reactive components without RxJS overhead.
    Clearer data flow in views.
  • Improved SSR support Better tooling for server-side rendering.
    More stable behavior in hybrid applications.
    Improved SEO and faster first paint.
  • TypeScript 5.2+ required The framework leverages newer TS capabilities.
    Better compatibility with the modern ecosystem.
    Fewer issues from compiler version differences.

Deprecated Features

  • Zone-dependent change detection assumptions deprecated
  • Legacy server rendering APIs phased out
// Zoneless configuration bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [provideExperimentalZonelessChangeDetection()] });
🔋 Zoneless

Angular 19 Modern Framework Era

Latest Innovations

  • Zoneless by default New apps start without Zone.js by default.
    Less hidden magic in change detection.
    Simpler performance analysis for components.
  • Improved Angular DevTools More accurate component rendering profiles.
    Faster identification of performance regressions.
    Better day-to-day debugging support.
  • Enhanced form validation Validation flows are more flexible and readable.
    Easier to build user-facing error messages.
    Less repetitive form code.
  • Better performance metrics Better insight into real rendering costs.
    Easier monitoring of post-release regressions.
    Simpler prioritization of optimization work.

Deprecated Features

  • Explicit Zone.js bootstrap configuration deprecated
  • Old test zone helpers replaced by modern defaults
import { provideZonelessChangeDetection } from '@angular/core'; bootstrap config uses stable zoneless configuration by default now
🌟 Angular 19

Angular 20 Future Ready

Latest Release

  • Enhanced performance optimization The engine takes better advantage of modern browsers.
    Critical view rendering becomes faster.
    The app scales better under heavy traffic.
  • Improved developer tooling CLI and debugging tools are more integrated.
    Issues are diagnosed faster during development.
    The daily workflow is less frustrating.
  • Advanced signal utilities Easier modeling of complex state dependencies.
    Less manual wiring of reactive logic.
    Better organization of domain code.
  • Future web standards support The framework supports new web standards faster.
    Your project is better prepared for upcoming years.
    Fewer costly migrations in the future.

Deprecated Features

  • Fallback compatibility mode APIs deprecated
  • Remaining legacy rendering hooks marked deprecated
// Angular 20+ ng version // Angular: 20.0.0+ // Ready for next decade
🚀 Angular 20+
Michał Postek, Copyright © 2019
